title: Kevin Warsh announces a monetary revolution at the Fed during Sintra forum
summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh indicated that inflation risks have diminished and outlined a new direction for monetary policy, generating mixed reactions in global markets.

title: Eurozone inflation falls to 2.8% in June and Lagarde rules out further rate hikes
summary: Consumer prices in the eurozone moderated more than expected, leading the ECB president to dismiss the possibility of further rate hikes.

title: Semiconductor stocks plummet at the start of the third quarter on Wall Street
summary: Shares of Micron, Corning and KLA record sharp declines, leading a broad downturn in the technology sector on the first trading day of July.

title: SpaceX reaches two trillion dollar valuation and denies AI smartphone prototype
summary: Elon Musk's space company joins the exclusive club of companies valued at over 2 trillion dollars, while the founder denies having shown a prototype of a smartphone with artificial intelligence before the IPO.

title: Micron signs long-term memory supply contract with General Motors
summary: Amid the chip shortage, GM secures supply of memory and storage platforms for its vehicles through a strategic agreement with Micron.

title: SanDisk shares plummet in trading session
summary: The storage manufacturer suffers a sharp stock market decline, though the immediate cause is unspecified.

title: Meta to launch cloud business to sell its excess artificial intelligence capacity
summary: Facebook's parent company will leverage its AI infrastructure to offer cloud services to third parties, boosting its shares.

title: Swedish court orders Google to pay $2 billion to Klarna for anti-competitive practices
summary: The tech giant is convicted of favoring its own payment services in search results, harming the Swedish platform Klarna.

title: Gold heads for its worst quarter in over a decade and continues to fall
summary: The precious metal extends its bearish trend due to the end of retail frenzy and uncertainty about interest rates.

title: Wall Street closes its best quarter since 2020 with record gains
summary: U.S. indexes recorded their largest quarterly advance in six years, driven by economic optimism and easing inflation.

title: Only a minority of cryptocurrency platforms obtain a license in the EU after new rules
summary: The new European regulations require a license to operate with crypto assets, but only a handful of companies have obtained one so far.

title: Bank of Japan intensifies warnings on yen and expectations of intervention grow
summary: Japanese authorities warn about the inflationary impact of the yen's weakness, while the market speculates on possible foreign exchange intervention.

title: Ibex 35 holds above 19,400 points after Warsh's speech in Sintra
summary: The Spanish index manages to hold above the 19,400 level in a directionless session following the statements by the Fed chair.

title: Microsoft faces key resistance after its worst month in decades
summary: The tech giant's shares struggle to break above the 394-408 dollar resistance zone after recording sharp monthly losses, generating interest among value investors.

title: Rise of PIK loans and private credit raises alarm over systemic risks
summary: Analysts warn that the growing popularity of payment-in-kind (PIK) loans and the expansion of private credit could become a time bomb for the financial system.

title: DAX surpasses 25,000 points at close, driven by defensive stocks
summary: The German index reaches a new milestone by surpassing the 25,000 barrier, with buying in the defensive and defense sectors.

title: Mixed data in the US: manufacturing activity weakens while labor market accelerates
summary: The manufacturing ISM index fell in June, but employment figures show a reacceleration, generating uncertainty about the direction of the U.S. economy.

title: BYD could snatch global electric vehicle leadership from Tesla in the second quarter
summary: China's BYD is close to overtaking Tesla in global sales for the quarter, while Tesla shares test resistance levels after a rally.

title: Telefónica loses support levels while IAG and Santander lead the Ibex's best quarter since 2020
summary: Telefónica shares fall below key levels, while IAG, Acerinox, and Santander gain more than 28% in the quarter, boosting the Ibex 35.

title: Euribor continues to fall as ECB considers doubling minimum bank reserves
summary: The downward trend in Euribor consolidates, while the European Central Bank weighs increasing mandatory reserves to ease financing costs for institutions.
